feat(types,serialize,codegen): add AssignTargetKind authority side table (#821)

* feat(types,serialize,codegen): add AssignTargetKind authority side table

- Checker populates assign_target_kinds in TypeCheckOutput for each
  Stmt::Assign, classifying the target as LocalVar, ActorField,
  FieldAccess, or Index.
- Serialized to msgpack as assign_target_kinds array (schema v3 -> v4).
- C++ ast_types.h and msgpack_reader.cpp updated to parse the new field.
- MLIRGenStmt.cpp generateAssignStmt now calls requireAssignTargetKindOf
  at entry, failing closed instead of falling through ad-hoc branches.
- Focused tests: checker side-table population and msgpack wire/builder
  coverage; C++ build remains gated on the unavailable cmake/ninja toolchain.

Refs: assign-target-authority slice PR A

* Sync generated msgpack reader

* Validate assign target authority metadata

* Parse assign_target_kinds in msgpack reader

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: check trait default method bodies for assign_target_kinds

Type checker skipped Item::Trait in check_item, leaving assign_target_kinds
unpopulated for assignments inside trait default method bodies.  MLIR codegen
uses requireAssignTargetKindOf (fail-closed) for every assignment, so the
missing entry caused a hard error on the bump() default in
param_mutability_codegen.hew.

Fix: add check_trait_defaults, which constructs a synthetic FnDecl for each
default method body and runs check_function_as on it.  This binds params into
env so Expr::Identifier targets resolve to LocalVar and get an entry in the
assign_target_kinds side-table.

Also add assign_target_kinds as the 7th required field in
packWithSchema / drop_funcs_roundtrip / method_call_receiver_kinds_roundtrip
in test_msgpack_reader.cpp; parseMsgpackAST uses mapReq so any minimal-valid
program fixture must include it.

commit.message:
fix(maglev): hoist keyed store address computation, fix store_bool bug

The store_bool path clobbered R11 (element address) with JIT_FALSE
before writing the bool payload, causing writes to garbage memory.
Fixed by hoisting address computation into R10 before the value type
dispatch, so both store_smi and store_bool branches share the same
pre-computed element address. Also removes redundant emit_load calls
for key and value in both store paths.

watchdog: fix fractional timestamp parsing in daemon_recently_active()

journalctl --output=short-unix produces timestamps like 1234567890.123456
on some systemd versions. The regex ^[0-9]+\$ rejects the decimal point,
causing daemon_recently_active() to always return false. The busy-streak
guard (rpc_busy_streak) therefore never fires and every RPC timeout goes
straight to fail_streak, triggering unnecessary restarts of a live node.

Fix: strip fractional seconds with \ before the regex check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add CodeQL config to exclude .vscode-test/ from analysis (#5456)

The .vscode-test/ directory is populated at CI test runtime by the
vscode-test npm package, which downloads a VS Code binary to run
integration tests. This directory is already in .gitignore and
contains third-party VS Code/extension code (not PowerShell source).

S360/CodeQL was flagging CodeQL.SM04514 'Weak hashes' in:
  .vscode-test/.../ms-vscode.js-debug/src/bootloader.js

That file belongs to the VS Code JavaScript Debugger extension
(ms-vscode.js-debug), owned by the VS Code team. Adding a CodeQL
paths-ignore config prevents the scanner from analyzing runtime
artifacts that are outside PowerShell's ownership and control.

Resolves: ADO #34872363

commit.message:
fix: auto-retry with resumeSession when agent evicts stale cached session

When the Copilot agent subprocess drops a session from its internal map
(due to inactivity/memory pressure), our cached CopilotSession object
becomes stale. Previously this caused permanent "Session not found"
errors until server restart.

Now _doWork() catches "Session not found" from session.send(), evicts
the stale cache entry, calls resumeSession() to re-register with the
agent, and retries the send — self-healing without restart.

fix: use ReactDOM.render instead of createRoot for CDN compatibility

createRoot is not reliably available in React 18 UMD builds from CDN.
Switched to ReactDOM.render which all presentations use successfully.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): update array IC after generic keyed store instead of invalidating

Previously, out-of-bounds keyed stores (e.g. sieve[i]=true during
array fill) would invalidate the inline array IC, forcing the next
access to miss and re-enter the Rust slow path. With n=1000 this
meant ~1000 consecutive IC misses during the sieve fill loop.

Now the generic store stub writes the updated (handle, data_ptr, len)
back to the caller's IC slots after a successful store. Subsequent
inline fast-path checks can hit immediately, even when the backing
Vec has been reallocated.

Add oneCCL groupStart/End for single P2P ops (#3071)

Due to recent changes from
https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/175712, bug was exposed showing
that single P2P ops hang. Although we expect oneCCL to support this, a
workaround is to add a group around send/recv ops if there is no current
active group.

This was causing hangs in `test/distributed/pipelining/test_stage.py`.
See https://jira.devtools.intel.com/browse/PYTORCHDGQ-8452 for test
details

commit.message:
fix: three Windows installer bugs — double-run, stale clone, theme never written

Bug 1: Switch double-run
  PowerShell switch is case-insensitive by default, so typing 'A' matched
  BOTH the "A" and "a" cases, running the entire workflow twice.
  Fix: switch -CaseSensitive + merged A/a into a single pattern case
  ($_ -in "A","a") so either capitalisation triggers one run.

Bug 2: Stale clone missing new files
  Symlink-Dotfiles only called git clone when the target directory was
  absent.  If the user had a clone from before themes/windows/ and
  windows/settings.json were added, those files were never downloaded.
  Fix: git pull --ff-only when the directory already exists (both the
  "dotfilesDir was null" and "dotfilesDir was known" code paths now pull).

Bug 3: Theme and WT colour scheme never written without repo files
  Configure-WindowsTerminal: fell back to font-only when settings.json
  was absent.  Now embeds the full Catppuccin Mocha colour scheme as a
  PSCustomObject so font + scheme are always applied to WT settings.json
  regardless of whether the repo clone is available.
  Configure-PowerShell: only deployed theme.ps1 when the repo catppuccin-
  mocha.ps1 existed.  Now writes the full theme inline (matching the repo
  file byte-for-byte) when the repo file is missing, so PSReadLine always
  gets Catppuccin Mocha colours on first run.
